extra.message: User input from the 'username' query parameter is concatenated directly into a Jinja2 template string via Jinja2.from_string("Welcome " + username + "!").render(). An attacker can inject Jinja2 template expressions (e.g., {{ ''.__class__.__mro__[1].__subclasses__() }}) to achieve remote code execution. The safe implementation using template variables is shown in the commented-out line 17.

User-controlled input (username query parameter) is rendered into an HTML response via HTMLResponse without any escaping or sanitization. An attacker can inject arbitrary JavaScript (e.g., <script>alert(document.cookie)</script>) by setting the username parameter. Combined with the SSTI on line 14, this also allows HTML injection even without Jinja2 syntax.
